diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, res, imp.Config.To))\n\n\t\t\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, keyData, allowUnknown)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tknownImports.Put(res, importID)\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: expanded import target %s\", res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// filter out any known import which already exist in state\n\tfor _, el := range knownImports.Elements() {\n\t\tif state.ResourceInstance(el.Key) != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] expandResourceImports: skipping import address %s already in state\", el.Key)\n\t\t\tknownImports.Remove(el.Key)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n}\n\n// validateExpandedImportTargets checks that all expanded imports correspond to\n// a configured instance.\n//\n// This function is only called from within the dynamicExpand method, the\n// import validation is inlined within the dynamicExpandPartial method for the\n// alternate code path.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validateExpandedImportTargets(expandedImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], expandedInstances addrs.Set[addrs.Checkable]) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tfor _, addr := range expandedImports.Keys() {\n\t\tif !expandedInstances.Has(addr) {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\", addr),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath == \"\" && len(n.importTargets) == 1 {\n\t\t\t// we have no config and aren't generating any. This isn't caught during\n\t\t\t// validation because generateConfigPath is only a plan option. If we\n\t\t\t// got this far however, it means this node is eligible for config\n\t\t\t// generation, so suggest it to the user.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import target %s does not exist. If you wish to automatically generate config for this resource, use the -generate-config-out option within terraform plan. Otherwise, make sure the target resource exists within your configuration. For example:\\n\\n  terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf\", n.Addr),\n\t\t\t\tSubject:  n.importTargets[0].Config.To.Range().Ptr(),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// ephemeral resources don't exist in state\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\n\n\t// Lock the state while we inspect it\n\tsMgr := ctx.State()\n\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\n\n\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, m := range moduleInstances {\n\t\t\tif m.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// The module instance of the resource in the state doesn't exist\n\t\t// in the current config, so this whole resource is orphaned.\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\torphans = append(orphans, res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tsMgr.Unlock()\n\n\treturn orphans\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar g Graph\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\n\n\tfor _, res := range orphans {\n\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n\t\t\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\t\tabs := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(addr)\n\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n\t\t\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\n\t\t\tg.Add(n)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now\n\t// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all\n\t// instances of the module.\n\t//\n\t// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way\n\t// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should\n\t// be expecting check results for, below.\n\n\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n\t\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\tfor _, instance := range instances {\n\t\t\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\n\n\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\n\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\n\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\n\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\n\t// visiting the checks.\n\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n\n\treturn &g, diags\n}\n\n// expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\n// itself in the context of a particular module instance.\n//\n// It has several side-effects:\n//   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\n//   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \"expander\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\n//   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\n//\n// After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\n// within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\n// checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\n// object instances for this resource instance.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n\n\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n\t// to work below.\n\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\n\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\n\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\n\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\n\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\n\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n\n\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\n\t// an index.\n\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\n\t// If there's only one instance then we might still need an index, if the\n\t// instance address has one.\n\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\n\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n\t}\n\tif mustHaveIndex {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n\t}\n\t// NOTE: The actual interpretation of n.forceReplace to produce replace\n\t// actions is in the per-instance function we're about to call, because\n\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\n\n\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\n\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\n\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\n\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges to incorporate into our\n\t// main graph which contains all of the resource instances together.\n\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\n\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n\n\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\n\t\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\n\t\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\n\t\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\n\t\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\n\t\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\n\t\treturn &Graph{}, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full state, so we'll\n\t// temporarily lock it while we work on this.\n\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\n\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\n\n\t// Start creating the steps\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\n\t\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResource(ctx, imports, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), n.skipPlanChanges),\n\t\t\tSchema:        n.Schema,\n\t\t\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\n\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add the count/for_each orphans\n\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResourceOrphan,\n\t\t\tAddr:          addr,\n\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n\t\t\tState:         state,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\n\n\t\t// Targeting\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Make sure there is a single root\n\t\t&RootTransformer{},\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the graph\n\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: steps,\n\t\tName:  \"nodeExpandPlannableResource\",\n\t}\n\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\n\n\treturn graph, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResource(ctx EvalContext, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], skipPlanChanges bool) func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\treturn func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\tvar m *NodePlannableResourceInstance\n\n\t\t// If we're in legacy import mode (the import CLI command), we only need\n\t\t// to return the import node, not a plannable resource node.\n\t\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\n\t\t\tif importTarget.LegacyAddr.Equal(a.Addr) {\n\n\t\t\t\t// If we're in the legacy import mode, then we should never\n\t\t\t\t// see unknown imports. If it is, we'll set the\n\t\t\t// import target to an unknown value so that the import operation\n\t\t\t// will be deferred.\n\t\t\tfor _, unknownImport := range unknownImports.Elems {\n\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Key.MatchesInstance(a.Addr) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Value.Has(a.Addr) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// This means that this particular instance already\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// exists within the state. `import` blocks that target\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// instances that already exist are ignored by\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Terraform. This means that even if this unknown\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// import does eventually resolve to this instance then\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// it would be ignored anyway. So for this instance we\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// won't set the import target.\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tm.importTarget = cty.UnknownVal(cty.String)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn m\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResourceOrphan(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\n\ta.Config = n.Config\n\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.skipRefresh,\n\t\tskipPlanChanges:              n.skipPlanChanges,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which\n//     represents the desired state.\n//\n//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because\n//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning\n//     to take against a particular resource instance.\ntype PlanGraphBuilder struct {\n\t// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.\n\tConfig *configs.Config\n\n\t// State is the current state\n\tState *states.State\n\n\t// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables\n\t// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part\n\t// of the plan walk.\n\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n\n\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n\t// initialization step.\n\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n\n\t// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and\n\t// provisioners) available for use.\n\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n\n\t// Targets are resources to target\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have\n\t// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace\n\t// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.\n\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources\n\tskipRefresh bool\n\n\t// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which\n\t// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the\n\t// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing\n\t// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to\n\t// resource instances. (This is for the \"refresh only\" planning mode,\n\t// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)\n\tskipPlanChanges bool\n\n\tConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n\tConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc\n\n\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n\tOperation walkOperation\n\n\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n\t// the actual graph.\n\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n\n\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n\t// framework.\n\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n\n\t// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.\n\tImportTargets []*ImportTarget\n\n\t// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n\t// from state without destroying.\n\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n\t// from state without destroying.\n\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n\n\t// GenerateConfig tells Terraform where to write and generated config for\n\t// any import targets that do not already have configuration.\n\t//\n\t// If empty, then config will not be generated.\n\tGenerateConfigPath string\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] building graph for %s\", b.Operation)\n\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n\t\tName:  \"PlanGraphBuilder\",\n\t}).Build(path)\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n\tswitch b.Operation {\n\tcase walkPlan:\n\t\tb.initPlan()\n\tcase walkPlanDestroy:\n\t\tb.initDestroy()\n\tcase walkValidate:\n\t\tb.initValidate()\n\tcase walkImport:\n\t\tb.initImport()\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(\"invalid plan operation: \" + b.Operation.String())\n\t}\n\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config\n\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: b.ConcreteResource,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t\tdestroy:  b.Operation == walkDestroy || b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\n\t\t\timportTargets: b.ImportTargets,\n\n\t\t\t// We only want to generate config during a plan operation.\n\t\t\tgenerateConfigPathForImportTargets: b.GenerateConfigPath,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add dynamic values\n\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n\t\t},\n\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n\t\t},\n\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{\n\t\t\tvalidateWalk: b.Operation == walkValidate,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:      b.Config,\n\t\t\tRefreshOnly: b.skipPlanChanges || b.preDestroyRefresh,\n\t\t\tDestroying:  b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t\tOverrides:   b.Overrides,\n\n\t\t\t// NOTE: We currently treat anything built with the plan graph\n\t\t\t// builder as \"planning\" for our purposes here, because we share\n\t\t\t// the same graph node implementation between all of the walk\n\t\t\t// types and so the pre-planning walks still think they are\n\t\t\t// producing a plan even though we immediately discard it.\n\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add nodes and edges for the check block assertions. Check block data\n\t\t// sources were added earlier.\n\t\t&checkTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:    b.Config,\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add orphan resources\n\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: b.ConcreteResourceOrphan,\n\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t\tskip:     b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// We also need nodes for any deposed instance objects present in the\n\t\t// state, so we can plan to destroy them. (During plan this will\n\t\t// intentionally skip creating nodes for _current_ objects, since\n\t\t// ConfigTransformer created nodes that will do that during\n\t\t// DynamicExpand.)\n\t\t&StateTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcreteCurrent: b.ConcreteResourceInstance,\n\t\t\tConcreteDeposed: b.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed,\n\t\t\tState:           b.State,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// add providers\n\t\ttransformProviders(b.ConcreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n\n\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. This must\n\t\t// come after all other transformers that create nodes representing\n\t\t// objects that can belong to modules.\n\t\t&ModuleExpansionTransformer{Concrete: b.ConcreteModule, Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Plug in any external references.\n\t\t&ExternalReferenceTransformer{\n\t\t\tExternalReferences: b.ExternalReferences,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\n\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Make sure data sources are aware of any depends_on from the\n\t\t// configuration\n\t\t&attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// DestroyEdgeTransformer is only required during a plan so that the\n\t\t// TargetsTransformer can determine which nodes to keep in the graph.\n\t\t&DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{\n\t\t\tskip: b.Operation != walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Target\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: b.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during apply.\n\t\t&ForcedCBDTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Close any ephemeral resource instances.\n\t\t&ephemeralResourceCloseTransformer{skip: b.Operation == walkValidate},\n\n\t\t// Close opened plugin connections\n\t\t&CloseProviderTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Close the root module\n\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Perform the transitive reduction to make our graph a bit\n\t\t// more understandable if possible (it usually is possible).\n\t\t&TransitiveReductionTransformer{},\n\t}\n\n\treturn steps\n}\n\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) initPlan() {\n\tb.ConcreteProvider = func(a b.preDestroyRefresh,\n\t\t\tforceReplace:         b.ForceReplace,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteResourceOrphan = func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn b.skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t\tforgetResources:              b.forgetResources,\n\t\t\tforgetModules:                b.forgetModules,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tb.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed = func(a key,\n\n\t\t\tskipRefresh:     b.skipRefresh,\n\t\t\tskipPlanChanges: b.skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t\tforgetResources: b.forgetResources,\n\t\t\tforgetModules:   b.forgetModules,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (b Even though the resource instances themselves are\n// already expanded from the plan, we still need to expand the\n// NodeApplyableResource nodes into their respective modules.\ntype nodeExpandApplyableResource struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResource\n\n\tPartialExpansions []addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandApplyableResource)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandsInstances() {\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\trefs := n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\n\n\t// The expand node needs to connect to the individual resource instances it\n\t// references, but cannot refer to it's own instances without causing\n\t// cycles. We also can't always just\n\t// filter them out for all resource node types, because the only method we\n\t// have for catching certain invalid configurations are the cycles that\n\t// result from these inter-instance references.\n\treturn filterSelfRefs(n.Addr.Resource, refs)\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) Name() string {\n\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\treturn n.dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx)\n\t}\n\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n\t\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, module)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)))\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil, diags\n}\n\n// We need to expand the ephemeral resources mostly the same as we do during\n// planning. There are other scenarios where the\n    23→// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\n    24→type ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\n    25→\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\n    26→\tConfig *configs.Config\n    27→\n    28→\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\n    29→\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\n    30→\n    31→\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\n    32→\t// and should not be applied.\n    33→\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n    34→\n    35→\t// State is the current state\n    36→\tState *states.State\n    37→\n    38→\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\n    39→\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\n    40→\t// to get a consistent result.\n    41→\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n    42→\n    43→\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n    44→\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n    45→\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n    46→\t// initialization step.\n    47→\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n    48→\n    49→\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\n    50→\t// provisioners) available for use.\n    51→\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n    52→\n    53→\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\n    54→\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\n    55→\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\n    56→\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\n    57→\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n    58→\n    59→\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\n    60→\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\n    61→\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\n    62→\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\n    63→\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n    64→\n    65→\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n    66→\tOperation walkOperation\n    67→\n    68→\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n    69→\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n    70→\t// the actual graph.\n    71→\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n    72→\n    73→\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n    74→\t// framework.\n    75→\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n    76→}\n    77→\n    78→// See GraphBuilder\n    79→func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n    80→\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n    81→\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n    82→\t\tName:  \"ApplyGraphBuilder\",\n    83→\t}).Build(path)\n    84→}\n    85→\n    86→// See GraphBuilder\n    87→func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n    88→\t// Custom factory for creating providers.\n    89→\tconcreteProvider := func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n    90→\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n    91→\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n    92→\t\t}\n    93→\t}\n    94→\n    95→\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n    96→\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n    97→\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n    98→\t\t}\n    99→\t}\n   100→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. There are other scenarios where the\n// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\ntype ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\n\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\n\tConfig *configs.Config\n\n\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\n\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\n\n\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\n\t// and should not be applied.\n\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n\n\t// State is the current state\n\tState *states.State\n\n\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\n\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\n\t// to get a consistent result.\n\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n\n\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n\t// initialization step.\n\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n\n\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\n\t// provisioners) available for use.\n\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n\n\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\n\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\n\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\n\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\n\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\n\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\n\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\n\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n\tOperation walkOperation\n\n\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n\t// the actual graph.\n\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n\n\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n\t// framework.\n\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n\t\tName:  \"ApplyGraphBuilder\",\n\t}).Build(path)\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n\t// Custom factory for creating providers.\n\tconcreteProvider := func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":240}}}

Check block data\n\t\t// sources were added earlier.\n\t\t&checkTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:    b.Config,\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// add providers\n\t\ttransformProviders(concreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n\n\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. Since DynamicExpand is always called after Execute, this is\n\t// safe.\n\tfor i, state := range n.states {\n\t\tg.Add(&graphNodeImportStateSub{\n\t\t\tTargetAddr:       addrs[i],\n\t\t\tState:            state,\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(g)\n\n\t// Done!\n\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// graphNodeImportStateSub is the sub-node of graphNodeImportState\n// and is part of the subgraph. This node is responsible for refreshing\n// and adding a resource to the state once it is imported.\ntype graphNodeImportStateSub struct {\n\tTargetAddr       addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\tState {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Cannot import deferred remote object\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"While attempting to import an existing object to The determination of this depends\n// on the count argument given.\n//\n// Orphans are found by comparing the count to what is found in the state.\n// This transform assumes that if an element in the state is within the count\n// bounds given, that it is not an orphan.\ntype OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer struct {\n\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\n\tAddr          addrs.AbsResource           // Addr of the resource to look for orphans\n\tInstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addresses that currently exist in config\n\tState         *states.State               // Full global state\n}\n\nfunc (t *OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\trs := t.State.Resource(t.Addr)\n\tif rs == nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Resource doesn't exist in state, so nothing to do!\n\t}\n\n\t// This is an O(n*m) analysis, which we accept for now because the\n\t// number of instances of a single resource ought to always be small in any\n\t// reasonable Terraform configuration.\nHave:\n\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n\t\t// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more\n\t\t// deposed objects) will be taken care of separately\n\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tthisAddr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\tfor _, wantAddr := range t.InstanceAddrs {\n\t\t\tif wantAddr.Equal(thisAddr) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue Have\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If thisAddr is not in t.InstanceAddrs then we've found an \"orphan\"\n\n\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(thisAddr)\n\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer: adding %s as %T\", thisAddr, node)\n\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n","numLines":65,"startLine":1,"totalLines":65}}}
